title: Spread Period PASS: m=7 cosmos orbit period=16 = Pisano period π(7)
_fixture_purpose: Second PASS fixture. The Pisano period π(7)=16: the Fibonacci sequence mod 7 repeats with period 16. The QA cosmos orbit for m=7 has period 16 (48 states = 3 orbits of length 16). F^8=-I mod 7 (F^16=I, period exactly 16). This fixture is notable because the period for m=7 is 16, NOT 24 — demonstrating that the cosmos period depends on the modulus and cannot be assumed constant.

spread_period._pisano_verification: Fibonacci mod 7 first returns to (0,1) at k=16

_matrix_witnesses._note: F^8=-I mod 7 ≢ I; F^16=I → period 16 minimal ✓

spread_period._qalm_orbit_check: QA affine map for m=7 has cosmos orbit period 16 (verified: 48 states in 3 orbits of length 16)

wildberger_reference.theorem: Spread Polynomials and QA orbit period (Wildberger arXiv:0911.1025)
wildberger_reference.statement: QA cosmos orbit period = Pisano period π(m). For m=7: π(7)=16.
wildberger_reference.qa_form: For m=7: π(7)=16 = cosmos orbit period; 48 cosmos states in 3 orbits ✓

validation_checks.description: Fibonacci period mod m == claimed cosmos_period
orbit_type ∈ valid set AND period=1 ↔ singularity
